item.title: Libs hate this one simple trick
item.description:
Data Centers are not the issue (IMOP) Last week, the Box Elder County Commission unanimously approved Kevin O’Leary’s 9-gigawatt data center campus.
I’m a socialist - I just liked the hook. The drought is real. Nine Western states are projected to hit critical water conditions by September. Utah peaks at 56% by September with 0% snow water equivalent remaining. This is not a normal year. This is not a fixable-by-shorter-showers year. So when a 9 GW data center gets proposed, the instinct to panic is rational. Here’s the thing: even in the worst-case cooling scenario, this facility pulls about 30 billion gallons per year. That’s roughly 1.8% of Utah’s total water diversions. With modern closed-loop cooling, it’s closer to 0.2%.
So how is the water used? ~64% of every developed gallon of water in Utah goes to feed livestock. Alfalfa and hay alone consume 68% of all diverted water in the state. Pasture and corn silage take another chunk. Add it up and the overwhelming majority of Utah’s water grows feed for cows, sheep, horses, and dairy operations. Food people actually eat directly; vegetables, fruit, grain, accounts for a few percent. The economic return on this water? Hay and alfalfa generate about 0.2% of Utah’s GDP. That is the same contribution to the state economy as amusement parks. Utah is using 2/3 of its water for the equivalent revenue of Lagoon Theme Park.
29% of Utah’s hay harvest is exported overseas. About two-thirds goes to China. That’s roughly 325 billion gallons of Utah water per year, compressed into bales and shipped to feed Chinese dairy cows. The 9 GW data center everyone is panicking about? It uses 17 billion gallons in a typical year. The water we ship to China as hay would run 19 of these data centers.
The actual math: - Utah diverts ~1.66 trillion gallons of water per year - Agriculture: 82% - Alfalfa & hay alone: 68% of the state total - Exports to China: ~325 billion gallons (≈20% of all developed state water) - 9 GW data center (typical hyperscale): ~17 billion gallons (~1%) - 9 GW data center (efficient closed-loop): ~4 billion gallons (~0.2%) The Great Salt Lake is dying. Utah’s own scientists have traced its collapse to upstream agricultural diversions in the Bear, Weber, and Provo basins. Not to server farms. Not to lawns. To alfalfa. Meanwhile, alfalfa farmers pay water rates measured in cents per thousand gallons under 19th-century prior-appropriation law. Data centers pay full municipal rates. Guess which one has a financial incentive to conserve.
I’m not saying agriculture doesn’t matter. Farming jobs are real. Rural communities are real. Ranchers feeding their own cattle is a fundamentally different thing than baling water for export. There is a serious version of this argument that protects family farms while reforming what we grow and who we grow it for. But pointing at a data center while we put our water on a boat to Shanghai is not that argument. It’s misdirection. If we’re going to have a real fight about who gets Utah’s water in a drought, the question isn’t “data centers or no data centers.” It’s: why the fuck do we allow this? Visit pnw-firemap.org to learn more about how Fire and Water are impacting the Western U.S.
item.dc:creator: skillamonth

27 Man Tax: Dener Pacheco
“You cannot buy the revolution. You cannot make the revolution. You can only be the revolution. It is in your spirit, or it is nowhere.” - Ursula K. Le Guin So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, **Imgur Creator Spotlight is now in your hands!! Having trouble finding people I haven't already featured recently. Now I'm going to just do who YOU nominate. Tag me or DM me and I'll feature whoever you pick.**
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: My little faith in humanity restored

item.title: Why we don't need sky daddy in schools.
item.description: But if you are religious, you are free to worship in any of these ungodly hellholes. Just don't drag your faith into the public office. Here in Norway we got rid of the state church in 2007, meaning 100% separation from the state. No political power or influence what so ever. America however... Need I say more? But I know many young Americans want the pastors and priest gone from public office. Or at least stop poking their personal faith into the public eye.
item.dc:creator: Rastifan

item.title: humans are weird, durable space orcs
item.description: I remember this post of collections of people talking about how humans would be super weird or terrifying to aliens if it turned out we were way more durable and robust than your average extraterrestrial. the page that had these on them seems to be gone so I reuploaded them here

item.title: Smug Cops With Death Fetish
item.description: Next Month: "Here is why we should equip drones with a flamethrower & why it is good for the economy" FP Edit: Remember, once the infrastructure is in place, it will be too late. These infrastructures will get "upgrades" later just as how cops turned into full-fledged militarized kill mongers and... ...a machine can't be held accountable! It's happening in front of us, while people discuss aesthetics, optics, and other irrelevant bullshit about this and elected officials rather than actually look and listen to what's happening. Your politicians are on a fully-accelerated locomotive towards authoritarian surveillance state that will later get armed to become the monster you have only seen in movies. Wake up! Vote these fucks out immediately and do a general strike. Not just on a convenient Friday, do it until they stop. Bring this machine to a grinding halt!
item.dc:creator: TheLastSpaceman

item.title: GovBrief Today #460
item.description: Attorney and politician Robert F. Kennedy Jr. ignored established medical practice today and discouraged Americans from taking antidepressants. He and Donald Trump ran the same play when they said taking Tylenol during pregnancy causes autism. And while no serious doctor will tell you flavored vapes are safe, Kennedy's FDA also approved four fruit flavored vapes today after Trump pressured the commissioner. Then the very same agency killed safety research on shingles and COVID vaccines, not for the first time, because the findings didn't fit their politics. Kennedy's so-called Make America Healthy Again movement keeps doing the harm it promised to stop. Talk to your doctor before you change any medication, no matter what Kennedy said today. And don't give these charlatans more power in November. I've emailed 7,274 stories over 460 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your name at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com More context, clickable links, data, and pictures on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260505-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/activist-miller-charges [2] https://go.govbrief.today/un-iran [3] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-may05 [4] https://go.govbrief.today/fda-vapes [5] https://go.govbrief.today/fda-covid-shingles [6] https://go.govbrief.today/rfk-antidepressants [7] https://go.govbrief.today/nyt-eeoc [8] https://go.govbrief.today/sc-redistricting [9] https://go.govbrief.today/judge-doj-attorney [10] https://go.govbrief.today/allen-indicted [11] https://go.govbrief.today/homan-ny-surge [12] https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-watchdog [13] https://go.govbrief.today/va-investigate-employees [14] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-attacks-pope [15] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-airport-profit [16] https://go.govbrief.today/senate-ballroom [17] https://go.govbrief.today/illinois-investigate-ice Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
